Attrlbute Thresholds_ MechWarrior: DEX +1 RFL +1 Elemental: STR +2 , DEX -1, BOD +1 Fighter Pilot: DEX +2, RFL +2, STR -1, BOD -1 Scientist: INT -1 STR -1 Technician: DEX +1, CHA -1 Merchant: CHA +1 BOD -1 Laborer: BOD +1, INT -1 Tralts: Clan characters may not take the Land Grant; Owns Vehicle, Poverty or Wealth traits_ except dark caste characters (see Leaving the Clans_ below). Equlpment: Clan characters who operate within the bound: aries Clan   society not have personai possessions. Instead_ their Clan allows them equipment appropriate t0 their caste and assignment: Clan   player characters can have any equipment appropriate t0 their occupational fields and skills (with the gamemaster $ permission and following the rules for Equipment Ratings_ p. 1291. However, character who leaves his or her Clan must return the equipment: Clan character who ends the Life Path outside the service of the Clans purchases equipment per normal rules (see Leaving the Clans below). Path Restrictions: Severe path restrictions apply to Clan characters based on their birth status and caste These restric- tions are described at each stage under the Clan Characters heading: Clan characters who do not follow these restrictions are branded as dark caste (see Leaving the Clans). Washing Out of Training: Any event that results washing out of training means that the character failed warrior training because of some fault or shortcoming_ character can also vol: untarily leave training; with the same effects_ The results vary depending on the character'$ status If the character washes out of the Trueborn Creche, he or she moves o to Stage 2_ Clan   Apprenticeship Path: If the character washes out   of Sibko (either trueborn or   freeborn), she  gains the Stigma / Failed Warrior Trait and moves on to Stage 3 as Clan civilian. either case freeborn character reverts t0 his her original Social Standing Attribute minimum and proceeds as his or her birth caste dictates; trueborn character is assigned new caste based on his or her aptitudes the player can sim ply choose lower caste and adjust the character' Social Standing minimum accordingly- Leaving the Clans: Some Clansmen detect , effectively joining the ranks of the dark caste, character who leaves the Clans at any point in the Life Path by choosing path outside the path restriction rules Or through random event can pro ceed as an Inner Sphere character following the standard rules but that character " Social Standing Attribute minimum drops to In addition he or she will be ostracized from the Clans and acquires the Poverty and Stigma Bandit Caste (21 traits. The character also loses any of the following traits accumulated up to that point: Rank, Commission, Vehicle , Custom Vehicle , Well: Equipped or Well-Connected The character can take the Owns Vehicle and Wealth traits, though they must be acquired through the Life Path or purchased later per standard rules_
Civlllan Castes The people of the Inner Sphere see the Clans as martial society; living to fight and conquer. In truth, though militarism is central to the Clan way of Iife Clan warriors form less than one percent of the population Those deemed unsuitable for warrior life , or who flunk out Of warrior training_ are assigned t0 the four civilian castes_ The work of these laborers merchants scien tists and technicians allows Clan society to function_ By Inner Sphere standards, Clan civilians are second-class citizens. Though the people accepted warrior rule in the years following the Exodus Civil War; in many regards is Iittle differ- ent from the Inner Sphere' neo-feudalism Clan civilians work to support tne military: while the military protects the civilians The few civilians unable t0 accept this system have dropped out and formed an outcast group; known variously as the dark or bandit caste_ With most   civilian efforts going support the warrior caste life for civilians can be hard- ~proportionately more than in the Inner Sphere. Indeed, the desire of Clan civilians for better life was one of the driving forces behind the Crusader philosophy; pushing the warrior caste tor return to the per- ceived Eden of the Inner Sphere. As the Nova Cat and Ghost Bear civilian castes have discovered, however, the Inner Sphere is not the paradise they hoped tor.
Game Materlal Because Clan society is starkly different from that of the Inner Sphere (see The Clans_ 189, for  more information) , Clan characters follow some different rules for character cre ation, especially with regard to the Life Path. The first choice player must make is whether his Or her Clan character is trueborn or freeborn: Trueborn characters are created by the Clan breeding program to be the ultimate war riors_ using vitro fertilization and gestation artificial wombs. They may or may not actually become the warriors tney were born and bred t0 be. Freeborn characters are conceived and born in the natural way; and few become warriors_ In tact, some Clans prohibit freebirths from attaining warrior status Clan characters must then choose caste which influ- ences all other facets of character creation: warrior, scientist, technician_ merchant; laborer 0r dark (bandit) Trueborn charac ters must choose the warrior caste_ Primary Language: English Secondary Languages: None Attrlbute Minlmums: Warrior: SOC 6; scientist: SOC 5; technician: SOC merchant: SOC 3; laborer: SOC 2: dark: SOC Clan characters ignore Social Standing minimum modifiers in the Life Path unless the path is specifically Clan Path; in those cases the new minimum applies even if ower than the pre- vious minimum  These minimums also serve as maximums: at the end of the Life Path_ Clan character' $ Social Standing Attribute must be equal to his Social Standing minimum;
